gauge.notes: The current best live story is the MN DNR / USGS St. Paul site already used on the shipped East River Flats to Hidden Falls route. For this downstream metro trip, that same gauge is at least as defensible because it sits closer to the take-out end and the current app already supports the DNR live path.

thresholdSupport.notes: Official DNR ladder already exists for the St. Paul gauge (scrapable below 2,000 cfs, low 2,000-6,000, medium 6,000-30,000, high 30,000-40,000, very high above 40,000). That is good enough for a conservative metro-Mississippi implementation once coordinates are cleaned up.

candidates.notes: Fresh metro-Mississippi discovery from the second official DNR Twin Cities recommended trip. DNR explicitly names both endpoints and mileage, Hidden Falls is already a documented public launch in V2, and Saint Paul documents Harriet Island Regional Park with a public dock. I stopped at named-endpoint preservation rather than authoritative coordinate extraction.

candidates.route: Zumbro Falls to Hammond
candidates.blocker: MN DNR explicitly recommends this exact 7-mile main-stem day trip and the direct USGS Zumbro Falls gauge is a clean live story, but the best numeric support surfaced on this pass was still neighboring-reach mixed evidence rather than a clean exact-route ladder for the Hammond take-out.
candidates.retryCondition: Retry if MN DNR, USGS-adjacent guidance, or strong same-route evidence ties the Zumbro Falls gauge directly to a conservative numeric floor/range for the Zumbro-Falls-to-Hammond corridor, or if a fresh exact-route trip report publishes a defensible minimum/range.

gauge.notes: USGS 05374000 at Zumbro Falls sits directly at the put-in side of the DNR-recommended route and already underpins nearby shipped Zumbro coverage, so the live gauge pairing itself is clean.

thresholdSupport.notes: The strongest numeric evidence surfaced on this pass is still mixed-source neighboring-reach support: Miles Paddled Zumbro River II uses the same Zumbro Falls put-in/gauge and recommends 7-9 ft, with 12 ft treated as high by DNR map reference, but that trip continues well past Hammond to Millville. That is useful signal, not clean exact-route threshold guidance for the 7-mile DNR Hammond trip.

candidates.notes: Fresh Minnesota-first discovery from the unreviewed Zumbro Map 2 recommended day trip. Official route legitimacy is clean: the DNR PDF explicitly recommends Zumbro Falls to Hammond at 7 river miles and the map text names Village Park at Hammond as the water access. It stays below likely_addable because the threshold package is still an exact-start / longer-finish stitch rather than a clean Hammond-specific ladder.
